name: Mushrooms-Broccoli
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse and pat dry fresh mushrooms or drain canned mushrooms. Halve them. Set aside.
2. Rinse broccoli. Trim stems into 1-inch pieces. Cut tops into small florets. Set aside.
3. In a large wok or skillet, melt butter over medium heat. Add onions. Sauté for 2 minutes until translucent.
4. Add mushrooms and sauté another 5 minutes until they release their moisture and begin to brown.
5. Remove the sautéed mushrooms and set aside in a separate bowl.
6. In the same wok, add oil over medium heat. Add the reserved broccoli and garlic. Stir-fry for 3 minutes.
7. Add water chestnuts. Continue stir-frying for another 2 minutes until crisp-tender.
8. Blend cornstarch, sugar, salt, and ginger with soy sauce in a small bowl to create a slurry.
9. Dissolve the chicken bouillon cube in boiling water. Stir into the cornstarch mixture.
10. Pour the sauce over the wok contents. Return the sautéed mushrooms. Cook uncovered until the mixture boils, then reduce heat and simmer covered for about 5 minutes or until thickened and broccoli is just crisp-tender.
11. Adjust seasoning if needed with salt and pepper. Serve hot with Chinese noodles, if desired.
Yield: 8 servings
